claim: Scoop is the recommended first install for Windows CLI tool management -- it requires no admin rights, provides clean installs, and is the preferred install method for most developer CLI tools
evidence: 23.4k stars, listed as primary install method by bat, fd, delta, eza, dust, yazi, and 15+ other tools evaluated

claim: fzf (78.9k stars) is the single highest-impact general-purpose CLI tool -- it transforms any list into interactive fuzzy-searchable selection and enables Ctrl+R shell history search
evidence: Most starred CLI tool evaluated, universal recommendation across all sources, transforms shell usability for non-developer users

claim: delta (29.6k stars) transforms git diff output into syntax-highlighted, word-level diffs with a one-line .gitconfig change -- benefits both Claude and user automatically
evidence: v0.19.1 released March 22, 2026, active development, zero-friction setup as git pager

claim: zoxide (33.9k stars) replaces cd with frecency-based directory jumping -- type 'z tech-debt' instead of full paths
evidence: Active development (v0.9.9 Jan 2026), explicit Windows docs, eliminates path memorization entirely

claim: ntfy.sh (29k stars) is the highest-value single tool across all research streams -- it fills the critical gap of push notifications from Claude Code tasks to phone/desktop
evidence: Independently identified as highest-value by SQ-008 and SQ-009; 3 independent Claude Code integration guides document hook setup

claim: The biggest gap in the user's AI setup is not another coding agent but orchestration/parallelization tools and notification infrastructure
evidence: User already has top 3 agents (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI). Research found 70+ competing agents but none adding significant value vs orchestration tools

claim: Aider (42.3k stars) is the strongest complementary AI coding tool -- git-commit-per-edit, 130+ LLM support including local Ollama models, and auto-lint/test after every change
evidence: 4.1M installs, 15B tokens/week processed, supports Claude as backend, complementary workflow

claim: Claude Squad (6.5k stars) enables running multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel with isolated git worktrees, but requires WSL on Windows due to tmux dependency
evidence: Purpose-built for Claude Code, v1.0.17 March 2026, actively maintained

claim: lazygit (74.9k stars) is the strongest interactive git tool -- visual staging, interactive rebase, conflict resolution, commit graph -- all without memorizing git commands
evidence: Nearly 75k stars, one of most popular dev tools, multiple independent reviews, full Windows support via Scoop/Winget

claim: git-absorb (5.4k stars) automates fixup commit creation -- stages changes and automatically identifies which prior commits they belong to using patch commutativity
evidence: v0.9.0 Feb 2026, port of Facebook's hg absorb, available via Winget on Windows

claim: difftastic (24.7k stars) provides syntax-aware AST diffs across 30+ languages, showing only semantically meaningful changes and dramatically reducing code review noise
evidence: v0.68.0 March 2026, actively maintained, pre-built Windows binaries

claim: tsgo (@typescript/native-preview) delivers 10x faster TypeScript type-checking as a drop-in replacement for tsc --noEmit, with 2.9x less memory usage
evidence: Microsoft-backed, nightly builds shipping (v7.0.0-dev), CLI-compatible with tsc flags, zero risk for type-check usage

claim: next experimental-analyze is a built-in bundle analyzer already available in Next.js 16.1+ that requires zero installation -- discovered via GitHub discussion, not main docs
evidence: Part of Next.js core, Turbopack-aware, route filtering, import chain tracing, --output flag for CI

claim: Unlighthouse (4.2k stars) extends existing Lighthouse setup from single-page to site-wide auditing with automatic route discovery and parallel multi-page audits
evidence: Actively maintained, low-effort adoption via npx, complements existing Lighthouse scripts

claim: Oxlint type-aware mode is already installed (v1.56.0) but may not have type-aware rules enabled -- enabling provides 59/61 typescript-eslint rules running 20-40x faster
evidence: Part of OXC project, 14k stars, config change only -- not a new tool installation

claim: WezTerm (23.3k stars) is the recommended terminal emulator upgrade -- built-in multiplexer eliminates tmux need, Lua scripting enables programmable behavior, native Windows
evidence: Active development, first-class Windows, hot-reload config, remote multiplexing support

claim: Nushell (38.8k stars) represents a paradigm shift -- all CLI output is structured data (tables, records, lists) that can be filtered, sorted, and queried without text parsing
evidence: First-class native Windows support, v0.111.0 latest, eliminates 'parse text output' bug class entirely

claim: The Charmbracelet ecosystem (Gum, Glow, Mods, Freeze, VHS) is the most cohesive CLI tooling suite with consistent design language and strong Windows support
evidence: Multiple tools with 3k-24k stars, all Go-based, all Windows-native via Winget/Scoop

claim: Nerd Fonts (56k stars) are a prerequisite for most modern CLI tools -- without one installed, Starship/Oh-My-Posh/eza/Yazi show missing glyphs instead of icons
evidence: Required by Starship, Oh-My-Posh, eza, Yazi, superfile; 3600+ icons, 50+ patched fonts

claim: yq (15.1k stars) extends jq to YAML, XML, CSV, TOML processing -- essential for Next.js/Firebase workflows involving config files, Firebase rules, and GitHub Actions
evidence: Actively maintained (2167 commits), full Windows support via Winget/Scoop/Chocolatey

claim: Hurl (18.7k stars) enables declarative API test files with assertions and request chaining -- tests are version-controllable .hurl files with JSON output
evidence: v7.1.0 Nov 2025, Rust/libcurl, available via Scoop/Winget/Chocolatey/Windows installer

claim: Firecrawl (core: 96.8k stars) is the most powerful web-to-structured-data tool for AI research -- turns websites into LLM-ready markdown with JavaScript rendering and MCP server
evidence: Most starred tool evaluated (core), CLI via npm, MCP server for Claude Code integration, free tier available

claim: jc (8.6k stars) converts output from 75+ CLI tools into JSON, bridging the gap between text-output tools and structured processing
evidence: 3917 commits, actively maintained, Python-based with Windows support

claim: croc (34k stars) enables secure file transfer between any two computers with end-to-end encryption -- single binary, first-class Windows support
evidence: v10.3.1 Nov 2025, Chocolatey/Scoop, PAKE encryption with generated codes

claim: Approximately 60% of CLI tools released since 2023 are written in Rust, driven by Ratatui framework and Rust's cross-compilation making Windows support nearly free
evidence: Pattern observed across 140+ tools: bat, fd, delta, ripgrep, dust, bottom, Yazi, difftastic, tailspin, hyperfine all Rust

claim: SoNash's existing infrastructure (124 npm scripts, 66 skills, 18 CI workflows, custom hooks) makes replacement tools low-value -- the highest-value additions are additive, not replacements
evidence: Biome, Vitest, Vercel CLI, generate-react-cli all offered negative value due to migration costs exceeding benefits

claim: Ruflo (23.3k stars) is NOT recommended for SoNash despite impressive features -- its 60+ agents, vector DBs, and RL routing overlap heavily with existing SoNash infrastructure
evidence: 390 open issues, Windows as secondary platform, would replace or conflict with existing hooks/agents/skills/memory

claim: Claude-Peers-MCP (815 stars) fills a genuine gap in inter-session communication but is too new (2 days old, 5 commits, no license) and requires --dangerously-skip-permissions
evidence: Created 2026-03-21, Windows PR pending, tool name collision issue #6, no license specified

claim: AI agents will compose with each other within 12-18 months -- Warp multi-agent orchestration, MCP integration, and Claude-Peers-MCP point toward specialized agents coordinating autonomously
evidence: Pattern observed across Warp, Agent Deck, Claude Squad, Claude-Peers, MCP protocol adoption

claim: Local model support (Ollama) in Aider, Goose, and OpenCode could reduce Claude Code API costs to zero for routine tasks while keeping Claude for complex architectural work
evidence: Aider supports 130+ LLMs, Goose is Apache 2.0, OpenCode has 75+ providers

claim: type-coverage (2.6k stars) provides a trackable metric for TypeScript type safety (e.g., '98.7% typed'), catching implicit any from inference failures that ESLint misses
evidence: Stable v2.29.7, complements strict mode, low maintenance burden

claim: GitHub Copilot CLI (9.5k stars) complements Claude Code by providing GitHub-native operations -- issue triage, PR summaries, repo exploration -- with free tier via all Copilot plans
evidence: GA since Feb 2026, native Windows support via Winget, deep gh CLI integration

claim: bottom/btm (13k stars) is the recommended lightweight system monitor for Windows -- first-class Windows support, low CPU overhead (1-3%), available via Scoop/Winget
evidence: Last commit Mar 2026, very active, Windows is a primary target platform

claim: tailspin (7.7k stars) provides zero-config log highlighting -- pipe any output through tspin for instant colorized dates, IPs, URLs, severity levels without configuration
evidence: Last commit Mar 2026, Rust, available via Scoop, works with stdin/files/less

claim: Windows Terminal has hidden power features most users miss: session restoration, named command-line layouts, extensions (v1.24+), Kitty keyboard protocol (v1.25+)
evidence: Documented in Microsoft DevBlogs, 97k stars, pre-installed on Windows 11

claim: Atuin (24k stars) provides encrypted shell history sync between machines -- records exit code, duration, directory, session context in SQLite for searchable knowledge base
evidence: Rust-based, commercial company backing, works with bash/zsh/fish/nushell

claim: git-cliff (11.6k stars) generates changelogs from conventional commits with regex-powered custom parsers and Tera templating, available via npm/pip/Cargo/Winget/Scoop
evidence: v2.12.0 Jan 2026, actively maintained, GitHub/GitLab integration

claim: Graphite CLI (gt) enables stacked PRs with automatic dependency management -- gt create replaces manual branch+commit+push, gt submit pushes entire stack
evidence: Backed by company (Graphite), available via npm, documented in multiple dev blogs

claim: llm (Simon Willison, 11.4k stars) enables piping data through any LLM from terminal with SQLite-backed history -- complements Claude Code for multi-model data processing
evidence: Actively maintained, Python, 50+ model provider plugins, MCP client support planned

claim: The scoop install one-liner 'scoop install fzf bat fd zoxide delta' deploys the entire Tier 1 foundation in under 5 minutes with no admin rights
evidence: Verified from each tool's official install documentation; all available in Scoop default bucket

claim: dstask (1.1k stars) is the best-fit CLI task manager for SoNash -- git-native, markdown-based, YAML+markdown format mirrors existing session context patterns
evidence: Native Windows support, Go binary, automatically commits task data to git

claim: CodeRabbit CLI integrates with Claude Code for autonomous dev loops: Claude writes code, CodeRabbit reviews, Claude fixes -- MCP integration available
evidence: Commercial product with free CLI tier, documented Claude Code integration guide

claim: Clipboard Project cb (5.3k stars) provides unlimited named clipboards from CLI -- replaces single-slot clip command with multi-clipboard management
evidence: Native Windows via PowerShell installer, first-class support

claim: MCP integration is becoming the universal connector between AI tools -- Firecrawl, Firebase, Ruflo, Claude-Peers, and Goose all expose or consume MCP servers
evidence: Pattern observed across 5+ tools independently adopting MCP protocol for Claude Code integration

claim: Biome is NOT recommended for SoNash -- migration cost from working ESLint+oxlint+Prettier pipeline outweighs benefits of consolidation
evidence: SoNash has mature three-tool setup with custom configs, lint-staged, pattern compliance checks
